II is now inquired that the people
acquired a white elephant when
they voted the amendment to the
city charter providing for installing
water mains in the same manner in
which public improvements are
made. by assessing the improve
ments made up to the abutting
property.


Careful estimates show that Ii
will cost the property owners from
$50 to $3.50 per so foot lot to install
a 6-inch main. If this should prove
true the amendment will have the
effect OF curtailing the installation
of new mains. Under the olu sys-
TEN the city put in the mains when
ever a certain revenue was assured,
which made 1t possible for people
of limited means to secure water
supply at no great cost


The claim is made that 1t will be
doubly difficult to secure new mains
under the new order of things. as
owners of unimproved property viii
be likely to hesitate before signing
petition which will bring an addi-
tional cost to their property of from
$50 to 800 per lot.


No new pipe has been laid under
